content: The PCAF compliance recommendations follow a priority-based framework tailored to entity type and current maturity level. Priority 1 (Critical): For institutions with no PCAF adoption or no financed emissions reporting. Recommended actions: join PCAF as a signatory, appoint a PCAF implementation lead, begin with the 2-3 largest asset classes by exposure, use proxy data (DQS 5) initially and plan for improvement. Priority 2 (High): For institutions with partial coverage that need to expand. Recommended actions: extend coverage to additional asset classes, report aggregate financed emissions totals, begin DQS tracking, establish data collection processes with key investees. Priority 3 (Advanced): For institutions with broad coverage seeking to optimize. Recommended actions: improve DQS scores through enhanced data collection, extend scope coverage to include investee Scope 3, implement sector-specific intensity metrics, develop transition targets based on PCAF data. Recommendations are parameterized by entity type: banks should prioritize Part B implementation alongside Part A expansion; insurers should develop Part C methodology in parallel; asset managers should focus on depth of coverage within Part A. Each recommendation includes a suggested timeline and expected outcome.
